---
title: "Placeholder"
subtitle: "Placeholder"
author: "Luís Monteiro, Vitaliy Davydovych"
format:
  revealjs:
    +mermaid-format: png # png also works
    theme: dark
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    chalkboard: false
    css: techart-theme.css
    footer: "Technical Art - (Insert Title Here)"
    highlight-style: github-dark
    code-block-bg: true
    mermaid:
      theme: dark
---

## What is Version Control? {.center}

> Version control is the practice of **tracking and managing changes** to files over time.

A Version Control System (VCS) is the software tool that does this for your entire project — not just code, but assets, configurations, maps, and more.

---

## Repository {.smaller}

The **repository** (repo) is where your project's entire history is stored.

- **Remote repository** — hosted on a server (cloud or on-premise). The shared source of truth for the team.
- **Local repository** — a copy of the repository on your machine. Where you do your work.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-height: 3.5
%%| fig-align: center
graph TB
  A[("Remote<br/>Repository<br/>(Server)")] <-->|sync| B[("Local<br/>Repository<br/>Dev A")]
  A <-->|sync| C[("Local<br/>Repository<br/>Dev B")]
  A <-->|sync| D[("Local<br/>Repository<br/>Dev C")]
```

---

## Centralized vs Distributed

| | Centralized | Distributed |
|--|--|--|
| Works offline | ✗ | ✓ |
| Full local history | ✗ | ✓ |
| File locking | ✓ native | Varies |
| Binary file handling | ✓ strong | Varies |
| Disk usage (local) | Low | Higher |
| Industry standard | AAA studios | Indie / most dev |

---
